verification_gate: Tasks cover clean source build, reproducible package hash, signed provenance, isolated install, data migration, runtime behavior, rollback, disaster recovery, and no profile mutation as build proof.

clause_1_clean_source_build.surface: clean Ubuntu 26.04+ x86_64 build (env -i, only FOUNDATION-001 profile on PATH, no ambient toolchain shadow)
clause_1_clean_source_build.chain: envctl materializes consolidated LifeOS source (CONSOLIDATE-001 single Rust workspace) from PostgreSQL production branch via module_path projection -> Nix build against x86_64-unknown-linux-musl via meta exec on pure-Rust dirs (bypasses napi-rs/wasm exhaust)
clause_1_clean_source_build.clean_guarantee: no host rustc/cargo/glibc leak; dev linker (mold, FOUNDATION-002-D) explicitly NOT inherited so static output stays byte-reproducible
clause_1_clean_source_build.evidence: Architecture Blueprint §7 Nix+musl + Phase 4 Materialization; FOUNDATION-003-B clean-env resolution; FOUNDATION-002-D linker split

clause_2_reproducible_package_hash.pins: root flake.nix + flake.lock (MISSING today per state/nix_surface_report.json.root_flake -> closed upstream by FOUNDATION-003-A) + rust-toolchain.toml + Cargo.lock + pinned Yazelix source path:/home/flexnetos/FlexNetOS/src/yazelix
clause_2_reproducible_package_hash.acceptance: second Nix build from same pins yields byte-identical store hash (digest equality) + pinned nix-profile generation
clause_2_reproducible_package_hash.evidence: Architecture Blueprint Nix+musl deterministic compilation; FOUNDATION-003-C reproducibility chain; state/nix_surface_report.json

clause_3_signed_provenance.signing: content-addressed store hash (SHA-256 identity) + committed flake.lock + committed source pins bound into a keyring-signed SLSA-style attestation bundle
clause_3_signed_provenance.authority: STORE-001 external-secret / provenance authority class (OS keyring + committed source pins + attestation bundles); PostgreSQL stores references/provenance never raw secrets; redb never holds secrets
clause_3_signed_provenance.lineage: every artifact traces to source commit/PR (adopted_capabilities.source.csv)
clause_3_signed_provenance.evidence: runner provenance contracts / FOUNDATION-002-F ubuntu-latest CI; FOUNDATION-003-A provenance ledger; STORE-001 state_class_assignments[external-secret/provenance]

clause_4_isolated_install.surface: fresh isolated Ubuntu 26.04+ target: disposable container / clean $HOME, NO Nix daemon, NO /nix/store, NO glibc assumption
clause_4_isolated_install.acceptance: static-musl install-in-place folder runs with zero external dependency and zero hardcoded /nix/store RPATH on a machine that never had the build toolchain; reads confined to declared $APPDATA/lifeos/* scope
clause_4_isolated_install.evidence: Architecture Blueprint 'install-in-place' (nix bundle / AppImage / nix-user-chroot); redb Technical Report §7 zero-dependency musl release; CLAUDE.md Tauri FS scope $APPDATA/lifeos/*

classes: account.json -> accounts table one-time boot migration (insert then rename to account.json.migrated-<ts>, never delete; corrupt JSON -> app still starts)
embedded sqlx migrations 000N_*.sql run idempotently in tauri::Builder::setup() before any IPC (db_health degraded when applied < embedded; db_migrate recovers)

redb-durable source-blob store -> PostgreSQL BlobStore parity cutover via codedb_store_pg::PgStore under one SHA-256 identity; class moves off redb-durable ONLY after green live blobstore_parity.rs vs disposable PostgreSQL
clause_5_data_migration.no_loss: SHA-256 identity + source commit/PR lineage preserved; account.json renamed not deleted; verified-cutover invariant honored (STORE-001)
clause_5_data_migration.evidence: CLAUDE.md storage layer (account.json migration, db_health, db_migrate); STORE-001 durable-relational migration_path; ADOPT-001 BlobStore/PgStore/parity

boot_sequence: LifeOS opens as the primary UI
Yazelix initializes Zellij + Nushell in background
Ruvnet agents spin up on static ruvllm via .rvf files (single frozen model, 50+ MicroLoRA agents)
LifeOS connects via UDS or shared memory-mapped redb state files (no overlapping state)
storage init Storage::new -> migrate -> migrate_from_json; db_health status == 'healthy' (applied == embedded); refuse-to-start calm error on failure
clause_6_runtime_behavior.acceptance: UI mounts, background workspace live, >=1 agent reachable over UDS/redb, db_health healthy
clause_6_runtime_behavior.evidence: Architecture Blueprint Phase 4 §25-26 (LifeOS UI + Yazelix + ruvllm + UDS/redb); CLAUDE.md storage init sequence

clause_7_rollback.mechanism: meta git snapshot (multi-repo checkpoint) + nix profile rollback to prior pinned generation + archived-release-plan restore + re-pin prior frontdoor order + PostgreSQL PITR restore for any ran migration
clause_7_rollback.rollback_plan: Restore the archived release plan; build, profile, and deployment state remain unchanged.
clause_7_rollback.guarantee: prior generation always restorable: retirement is archive-first (no deletion) and blocked_paths forbid build execution / profile mutation / release publication / deployment
clause_7_rollback.evidence: task_graph.source.csv RELEASE-002 rollback_plan; FOUNDATION-003-J rollback pattern; meta-destructive-commands snapshot policy

recovery_by_class.transient_redb_state: transient-by-definition on loss; no durable recovery owed
recovery_by_class.per_agent_cognition_rvf: restore .rvf file or regenerate from base model + training history
recovery_by_class.derived_projections_and_release_artifact: re-projection from PostgreSQL + deterministic rebuild from committed pins (same content-addressed hash)

clause_8_disaster_recovery.guarantee: no derived release artifact is a single point of durable loss; durable authority is PostgreSQL alone
clause_8_disaster_recovery.evidence: STORE-001 state_class_assignments recovery_source fields (all six classes)

clause_9_no_profile_mutation_as_build_pr.profile_consumption: release build consumes FOUNDATION-001 Yazelix plus Nix foundation profile (proven to resolve by FOUNDATION-003-B) read-only as sole provider; correctness shown by consuming a fixed generation, not changing it
clause_9_no_profile_mutation_as_build_pr.definition_only_envelope: simulation_required=TRUE; each proof is a machine-checkable gate whose execution is a downstream release-cell task; this family executes no build, mutates no profile, publishes no release, deploys nothing

goal_item_coverage.ubuntu_26.04+_first_release_gate: release_gate_target (gatekeeper: no macOS/Windows until Ubuntu succeeds)

gate_result.store_001_alignment: Release layer maps onto STORE-001 without reclassifying any store: PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical durable (PITR); redb transient high-frequency (loss transient-by-definition); AgentDB .rvf per-agent non-authoritative cognition; materialized source/package/install prefix are derived-projections/derived-build (PostgreSQL canonical, envctl projection read-only, deterministically regenerable); source pins + attestation bundles are external-secret/provenance authority (keyring + committed pins); redb-durable CodeDB blobs migrate to PostgreSQL only via verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover. Building to intended specs is an upgrade, never a downgrade.

proof_summary: RELEASE-002 defines the Ubuntu 26.04+ x86_64 first-release gate as an 11-item task family (RELEASE-002-A..K), and every one of the verification gate's nine clauses is covered by exactly one work item (with clause 9 'no profile mutation as build proof' split across the profile-consumption item D and the definition-only build-proof envelope K), each grounded in named sources rather than invented. Clean source build (A) materializes the consolidated LifeOS Rust workspace (CONSOLIDATE-001) via envctl projection from the PostgreSQL production branch and compiles it against x86_64-unknown-linux-musl in a clean env with only the FOUNDATION-001 profile on PATH, deliberately not inheriting the mold dev linker so the static output stays byte-reproducible. Reproducible package hash (B) requires a second deterministic Nix build from the same pins to yield the byte-identical content-addressed /nix/store/...-lifeos hash, closing the missing root flake.nix/flake.lock gap recorded in state/nix_surface_report.json via FOUNDATION-003-A. Signed provenance (C) records rustc/cargo/target-triple/lockfile-hash/binary-checksums into manifests/provenance.json and binds the SHA-256 store hash + committed flake.lock + source pins into a keyring-signed SLSA-style attestation bundle owned by the STORE-001 external-secret/provenance authority class. Isolated install (E) proves the static-musl install-in-place folder runs on a fresh Ubuntu 26.04 target with no Nix daemon, no /nix/store, and no glibc assumption. Data migration (F) covers the three real durable-data classes: account.json -> accounts table (rename-never-delete), idempotent sqlx migrations gated by db_health/db_migrate, and the redb-durable CodeDB blob store -> PostgreSQL BlobStore parity cutover under one SHA-256 identity that only cuts over after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run (STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant). Runtime behavior (H) proves the intended boot: LifeOS UI opens, Yazelix initializes Zellij+Nushell, Ruvnet agents spin up on static ruvllm via .rvf, LifeOS connects over UDS/shared redb, and db_health returns healthy after account.json migration. Rollback (I) restores the prior generation via meta git snapshot + nix profile rollback + archived-plan restore + PostgreSQL PITR, leaving build/profile/deployment unchanged. Disaster recovery (J) restores each STORE-001 class from its named recovery source (PostgreSQL PITR, reproducible re-embedding, redb transient-by-definition, .rvf regeneration, deterministic re-derivation of derived artifacts, keyring+pins). The release_artifact_ledger enumerates eight release artifacts/state-classes, each assigned exactly once with owner, lifetime, forward-only sync edge, recovery source, and STORE-001 relationship, so PostgreSQL remains the single durable authority and redb/AgentDB stay non-durable. The upgrade item (G) preserves persisted state and the LIFEOS_PERSIST_KEYS whitelist forward-only with reversible fallback, retiring the prior generation archive-first only after the new one is proven. This is a definition/simulation task only (simulation_required=TRUE, execution_cell=release-cell): no build was executed, no profile mutated, no release published, and nothing deployed; all reads were non-mutating and product_execution_performed is false.
